about:
Welcome to my shelf. I've been collecting thoughts, stories, and sketches for years. Feel free to browse through the folders on the right. AND ENJOY!

Contact: veramg06@gmail.com

essays.title: The Art of Slow Living
essays.content: In a world that never stops moving, there is a profound power in standing perfectly still. This essay explores how silence can be a form of resistance...

journal.title: Random
journal.content:
Journal.J : Random 

Man 1: “Oh, I was expecting a fat person, heh.”

Man 2: *ignores* 

Then he points at me. I’m sitting in the last row of a taxi, squashed between these strangers, silent. His tone was smug, like I was part of some joke he never said out loud. But instead of leaning in with that same energy, he shifts away, plastering himself to the far edge of the seat, leaving a gap between us, like the fat person he was expecting was there. 

I take out my phone. I open the eBook I’ve been meaning to finish not to read, really, but to escape. It's been my classic move lately. Five minutes pass and I can’t get through a single sentence. Man 2 keeps making phone calls, loud and pointless, so I give up and eavesdrop to pass the time.

Man 2: “Ey, Besresh…”
(later I learned Besrsh was their guy friend, which I confused him to be a  woman at first)

He hangs up. Turns to his friend, Man 1, whose badly patterned shirt matches his black cape which nearly blends into his skin. Man 2 is by my left and is light skinned. He was trying to make small talks which were too small to talk.

Man 2 : Besrsh just had a baby (it was meant to be a news)

Man 1 : ..

Man 2 : oh shit, I forgot to ask the gender (he calls back)

Later, he says, “It’s perfect.” Hangs up again.

Man 1: “It’ll be a ‘chick.’ Once the 'chickes' start, they don’t stop. 'Chickes' are like that. His first was a 'chick'.”

He keeps saying chick. Like it’s his favorite word. For unexplainable reason laughter bubbles up in me and I try to kill it before it escapes. I glance at him, hoping it’ll put me back in check. That's when I noticed his tattoo.

His left palm catches my eye there’s a faded, green tattoo. It took me more than a glance to make a sens out of it. Homemade. Probably done by friends. I squint. My glance turned into stare. Finally I figured the middle part says "Rina". Curious itch scratched, I turn back to my phone.

Man 1 adds "so what's it?"

Man 2 : "yes, it's a girl" 

Man 1 : " then why did u say 'perfect' " 

Man 2 says "what was I supposed to say?" ,  Confused.


For that Man 1 smiles, proud his ‘chick’ theory was proven right. He keeps explaining it, even though none of it makes sense. He doesn’t stop until Man 2 reminds him it’s their stop.

They get out. Man 1 stares at me with a deadly stare for a good one minute till the taxi moves. Cold stare. I only just now notice his face. His clothes. The weight of something I can’t quite name.

journal.title: A Rainy Tuesday in Winter
journal.content: The rain hasn't stopped since morning. It's the kind of day where the tea never stays hot long enough and the ink flows a bit slower on the page.

stories.title: The Weaver of Mist
stories.content: High above the valley, where the air grows thin and cold, lived a woman who could weave the morning mist into threads of silver...

books.title: Meditations
books.author: Marcus Aurelius
books.review: A timeless collection of thoughts on virtue, mortality, and the human condition. It remains my most-referenced book.

paintings.title: Blue Ridge Twilight
paintings.description: An oil study of the darkening peaks at sunset. Focused on the transition from deep indigo to soft violet.
